name: Dragon NaturallySpeaking
short_description: The first consumer continuous speech-recognition software, letting users dictate text and control a PC entirely by voice.
long_description: Released in April 1997 by Dragon Systems — founded in 1982 by Dr. James K. Baker and Dr. Janet M. Baker — NaturallySpeaking 1.0 was the first commercial product to recognize naturally flowing, continuous speech without requiring users to pause between every word. Built on hidden Markov models and statistical language models, it achieved dictation speeds exceeding 100 words per minute at launch. For people with motor impairments, RSI, and other physical disabilities, it became a transformative assistive technology that unlocked computer access without any reliance on a keyboard or mouse — establishing voice as a serious, accessible input modality two decades before smart speakers made it mainstream.
ai_note: Hidden Markov model acoustic and statistical language models are central to what the system does — speech-to-text recognition is the product.

images.caption: Dragon NaturallySpeaking logo — the product's heptagon mark in black and green.
images.alt_text: The Dragon NaturallySpeaking logo: a dark green heptagon (seven-sided polygon) with the word Dragon in black lettering below it.
